user_query:
Please read the two medical research abstracts in input/abstract1.txt and input/abstract2.txt. For each abstract, generate a plain-language summary for non-experts that:
- uses the target audience and limits below (abstract1: audience="patients", max_words=180; abstract2: audience="media", max_words=120)
- replaces medical jargon with simple explanations in-line
- extracts exactly 3 key takeaways
- estimates the reading level (aim for Grade 6-8)
- logs the jargon terms you replaced and their plain explanations

Write the results as two JSON objects (one per line) to output/patient_summaries.jsonl with fields: lay_summary (string), reading_level (string), key_takeaways (array of 3 strings), word_count (int), jargon_replaced (array; each item should include the original term and its plain explanation). Make sure word_count equals the actual number of words in lay_summary and that lay_summary respects the max_words for each abstract. Also add a short methods note describing your simplification approach to output/README.md.

Do not include any absolute or system-specific paths; use only the input/ and output/ paths specified here.
